AF. — Agnes Faith · Finance & Data AnalyticsLewis University · Romeoville, IL
Projects

Work that matters

Quantitative finance, full-stack development, and data analytics projects built with Python, R, React, and machine learning.

Featured · Active Research
QuantCluster

Using PCA and K-Means clustering to inductively identify latent behavioral bias profiles among fixed-income investors. Investigates the 2024 DALBAR anomaly — why retail bond investors underperformed by 232bps while increasing fund contributions. Cluster profiles: yield chasers, momentum herders, inertia holders, rational rebalancers.

PythonPCAK-Meansscikit-learnCRSPMorningstarDALBAR

AF. — Agnes FaithLewis University · Romeoville, IL
Research

Current interests

Graduate research at the intersection of behavioral finance, machine learning, and fixed-income markets. Supervised by Prof. Apostolos Xanthopoulos at Lewis University.

Active Research
Behavioral Bias Profiles in Fixed-Income Markets

Applying PCA and K-Means to CRSP/Morningstar fund flow data to discover latent behavioral clusters among bond investors. Examines the 2024 DALBAR anomaly: why did average fixed-income investors lose 1.07%, underperforming the Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate by 232bps, while simultaneously increasing contributions?

PCAK-MeansCRSPDALBARBehavioral Finance
Interest
ML in Active Fixed-Income Management

Evaluating whether ML-based duration management models outperform passive strategies during rapid rate volatility cycles such as 2022–2024.

Machine LearningFixed IncomeDuration
Interest
Market Regime Detection via Hidden Markov Models

Using HMMs to identify latent market states — bull, bear, transition — and examining how investor behavior shifts across regimes in fixed-income fund flows.

HMMRegime DetectionFund Flows
Interest
GSE Accounting Reform & Systemic Risk

Extending prior literature on FNMA/FHLMC accounting standard delays to post-conservatorship reforms — examining whether structural transparency problems identified pre-2008 were resolved.

GSEAccountingFNMA
AF. — Agnes FaithLewis University · Romeoville, IL

Education
BSc Real Estate — 2nd Class Honours (Upper)

Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology, Kiambu, Kenya. Sep 2020 – Apr 2024. Studied property valuation, land economics, construction management, GIS, and real estate finance across the Kenyan market.

AF. — Agnes FaithLewis University · Romeoville, IL
About

Agnes Faith

MS in Business Analytics · Real Estate · Lewis University · Romeoville, IL

I am an MS in Business Analytics candidate at Lewis University and VP of the Business Analytics Club, with hands-on experience in data analysis, social media strategy, event coordination, and software development.

Currently conducting research on behavioral biases in fixed-income markets with Prof. Apostolos Xanthopoulos — applying PCA and K-Means to CRSP/Morningstar fund flow data to uncover why retail bond investors underperformed by 232bps while increasing contributions in 2024.

Multilingual in English, Swahili, and Italian with a cross-cultural perspective shaped by years in Nairobi and now Chicago. I bring a track record of independent project execution to every role.

Outside of research I track every film I watch and every song I stream — and build dashboards to make sense of it.
